review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-113-claude-review-output-valid.json

findings.title: audit_annotation.hint flows from evaluatePermission(resource=...) into the serialized response — verify it cannot echo hidden source fields
findings.evidence: auditAnnotation.hint = decision?.audit_hint ?? null is built from evaluatePermission(), which receives the full resource including the four omit-listed fields (privileged_note, cross_tenant_secret, internal_policy_label, sealed_audit_hint_payload). serializeResource() then places audit_annotation (an allowlisted field) into response_contract.items[0]. If evaluate.js copies resource.sealed_audit_hint_payload (or similar) into audit_hint, the omission guard is bypassed via the audit annotation channel. serializeResource itself omits the raw fields correctly, so the only residual channel is the evaluator's audit_hint.
findings.recommendation: Confirm evaluatePermission's audit_hint is a decision-shaped hint that never echoes raw resource source fields, or sanitize auditAnnotation.hint against hidden_source_fields before serialization. No change strictly required if evaluate.js is already field-bounded.

findings.title: Invalid-pagination response sanitizes page_size while pagination_filtering_contract echoes the raw rejected value
findings.evidence: For page_size=101, response_contract.page_size resolves via isValidIntegerInRange(pageSize,1,100) ? pageSize : 25 (→ 25), but pagination_filtering_contract.page_size returns the raw pageSize (101) with valid:false. Same rejected request is represented two different ways. Harmless (request is rejected, items:[], error_codes:[invalid_pagination]).
findings.recommendation: Document the requested-vs-applied intent or align the two fields to avoid consumer confusion.

findings.title: Filtering surface enforces pagination bounds only; no filter predicate is applied
findings.evidence: Surface/title 'pagination_filtering_contract' / 'Pagination or filtering contract' implements page/page_size bounds (page_min 1, page_size_max 100, invalid_pagination) but no filter parameter. Acceptable under the 'or' title and metadata-only Risk A scope.
findings.recommendation: Confirm any filtering semantics are intentionally deferred to CP00-114; no change required for this pack.

findings.title: Validator additions reference requireIncludes, not shown in this diff
findings.evidence: New assertions call requireIncludes(...) for api_binding_surfaces, error_codes, safety_codes, allowlist, and hidden_source_fields. The helper definition is outside the diff; if it is not already defined in the file the Hermes validation command would throw a ReferenceError.
findings.recommendation: Verify requireIncludes is defined in the existing validator scope alongside requireEqual/requireTrue/requireFalse.

parsed_review.rationale: Clean synthetic Risk A boundary pack; all in-scope guarantees hold. Pagination bounds 1..100 enforced with invalid_pagination; serialization is strict allowlist-only (serializeResource emits only resource_id/resource_type/tenant_id/matter_id/title/permission_annotation/audit_annotation) and the four hidden source fields are never serialized (tests + validator assert undefined); unauthorized_data_omitted safety code is always present (fail-closed). evaluatePermission is invoked only after synthetic, tenant, matter, and pagination prechecks pass (evaluator_invoked false for non-synthetic/cross-tenant/matter-drift/invalid-pagination, true for allow/deny). permission_denied and invalid_pagination are both exercised, plus non_synthetic_request_blocked, tenant_boundary_precheck_failed, matter_trace_precheck_failed. Audit annotation is preview_only with emitted_to_audit_ledger:false; all no_write_attestation flags (policy/audit/product/database/idempotency/locks/rollback/retry/AI/export/external-share/approval/claude/ldip) are false and the module performs no actual I/O. HRX stays embedded-only and handoff is fixed to CP00-114 / RP02.P03.M05.S17. Contract JSON, README, tests, and the validator are mutually consistent (10 units S07–S16, deliverable counts 2/3/3/1/1, Claude packet claude-opus-4-8 effort max read_only exactly_one). The single P2 is a verification item on the audit_hint channel, not a confirmed leak, and does not block closeout; remaining items are informational nits.
notes: No P0/P1 findings were reported by the valid CP00-113 pack-level Claude review.
Original P2 audit-hint channel finding was fixed after review by sanitizing the hint and adding test/validator assertions for hidden source field omission.

P3 invalid-pagination requested-versus-applied note is documented as non-blocking because rejected request metadata intentionally preserves requested values while response_contract uses safe applied defaults.
P3 filtering predicate note is documented as non-blocking because CP00-113 implements the pagination side of the "pagination or filtering" unit and CP00-114 continues the same Risk A boundary.
P3 requireIncludes note is documented as non-blocking because requireIncludes is already defined in the existing validator scope.
No second valid Claude review was run.
